topic: PRO registration: which societies African composers should join
hook: Every time a licensed placement airs anywhere in the world, royalties generate.

slides.eyebrow: Education · PRO registration: which societies African composers should join
slides.headline: Every time a licensed placement airs anywhere in the world, royalties generate.
Without PRO registration, they go uncollected — permanently.
slides.lede: Those royalties have to go somewhere. · Here's the definitive breakdown for African composers. ↓

slides.headline:
NIGERIA 🇳🇬
COSON — primary collection society for compositions
Also register
slides.lede: with: PRS for Music (UK), ASCAP or BMI (US)

slides.headline:
SOUTH AFRICA 🇿🇦
SAMRO — compositions and publishing royalties
CAPASSO —
Here's what you need to know:
slides.lede:
KENYA 🇰🇪
MCSK — Music Copyright Society of Kenya · GHANA 🇬🇭
GHAMRO — Ghana Music Rights Organisation

slides.headline: Here's what you need to know:
slides.lede:
FOR INTERNATIONAL SYNC SPECIFICALLY:
Every African composer with sync ambitions should also register: · PRS for Music (UK) — captures European royalties · ASCAP or BMI (US) — North American collection · SOCAN (Canada)
list_items:
FOR INTERNATIONAL SYNC SPECIFICALLY:
Every African composer with sync ambitions should also register:
PRS for Music (UK) — captures European royalties
ASCAP or BMI (US) — North American collection

slides.headline: You register with your home society — they collect internationally through

THE KEY RULE:
Register before your first placement.
slides.lede:
Not after.
Retroactive collection is almost impossible.
Royalties from a placement that aired before you registered: gone.

slides.headline: One track. Multiple airings. Royalties every time the content plays.
Open briefs. Every Tuesday.
slides.lede: Composers: apply once, get matched for life. Supervisors: shortlist in 48h.
